tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Open Google and search 'warm backpacking March USA'
tasks.hook_line: Weekend is in 2 days — nowhere to go yet.
tasks.reward: Locks in the adventure piece of Mariano's birthday celebration
nuggets: Big Sur warm in March, 60°F days
Joshua Tree good for beginner backpacking
Smoky Mountains can still be cold, check forecast
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for a 1-2 night trip this weekend (March 14-15, 2026). It's for my friend Mariano's 30th birthday. I'm based in the US and need somewhere I can fly to easily. I'm a backpacking beginner so I need established trails with clear routes. Warm weather is essential — ideally 55-70°F. Give me 3-5 destination options with trail recommendations, difficulty ratings, how to get there, and what gear I'd need. Include permit requirements if any.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and enter destination from Task 1
tasks.hook_line: 2 days out — prices climb every hour you wait.
tasks.reward: Backpacking trip becomes real — no backing out now
nuggets: Southwest often best for last-minute domestic
Thursday night flights usually cheaper
Budget $200-400 round trip domestic short notice
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book flights for a backpacking trip this weekend (March 14-15). Departing from [your home airport]. Going to [destination from Task 1]. I need to fly out Thursday evening (March 13) or early Friday morning (March 14), and return Sunday evening (March 15) or Monday morning (March 16) because I fly to Chicago Monday afternoon. Find me the cheapest options on Google Flights, Southwest, or Delta. Show me total costs and times.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search backpacking city to Chicago
tasks.hook_line: Chicago flights for March 19th birthday — book now or pay double.
tasks.reward: Full travel route locked — backpacking to Chicago seamless
nuggets: Monday afternoon or evening flight ideal
O'Hare bigger hub, usually more options
Budget $150-300 depending on backpacking location
tasks.system_prompt: I need a flight from [backpacking destination] to Chicago on Monday March 16 or Tuesday March 17, 2026. Mariano's birthday is Thursday March 19 so I need to be there by Wednesday latest. I'm flexible on time but prefer afternoon/evening so I can finish the backpacking trip in the morning. Find me cheapest options to either O'Hare or Midway. Show total costs and times.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Go to REI.com and click 'Rentals' in top nav
tasks.hook_line: REI rents full backpacking kits — never done this before.
tasks.reward: Gear question answered — no last-minute scrambling or buying expensive stuff
nuggets: REI Adventure Kit includes pack, tent, bag, pad
Reserve online, pick up in store
~$50-80 for weekend rental vs $500+ to buy
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out REI gear rentals for a 1-2 night backpacking trip this weekend (March 14-15). I'm a beginner and don't own backpacking gear. I need: backpack (50-65L), tent (1-person or 2-person), sleeping bag (rated for 40-50°F), sleeping pad. Find the REI Adventure Kit or individual rental options. Tell me what's included, how much it costs, how to reserve it, and what I'd still need to bring (clothes, food, etc.). Include pickup/return process.

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano: 'What do you want for your 30th?'
tasks.hook_line: What do you get someone turning 30 who already has everything?
tasks.reward: Shows up with more than just 'happy birthday' — thoughtful gesture
nuggets: Experience gifts good for milestone birthdays
Chicago restaurant gift card easy option
Trip itself might be gift enough
tasks.system_prompt: Help me brainstorm a 30th birthday gift for my friend Mariano. I'm already flying to Chicago for his birthday and doing a backpacking trip beforehand. Budget around $50-100. He's into outdoor activities, good food, and Chicago nightlife. Give me 5-7 gift ideas that are either tangible items, experiences, or gift cards. Include where to buy them and why they'd be good for a milestone birthday.

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto hits first — which optical shops open early in the trip?
tasks.reward: Glasses shopping starts immediately when you land in Japan
nuggets: JINS and Zoff are major chains, English-friendly
Kyoto station area has most optical shops
Glasses ready same day or next day in Japan
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan that are foreigner-friendly. I'm visiting March 25-27, 2026 and want to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses early in my trip. I need stores that: speak English or have English signage, can do eye exams with US prescription, have stylish frames, and offer same-day or next-day service. Give me 5-7 store names with Google Maps links, locations (near Kyoto Station ideal), price ranges, and what makes each one good. Include JINS, Zoff, and any local boutiques.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Open your insurance portal and search 'vision coverage'
tasks.hook_line: Can you claim foreign optical purchases on US insurance?
tasks.reward: Know exactly what to ask for at checkout — receipts, codes, prescriptions
nuggets: Most US vision insurance covers foreign receipts
Need itemized receipt with frame/lens breakdown
Bring current prescription or get eye exam in Japan
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out how to buy prescription glasses in Japan and potentially get reimbursed by my US vision insurance. Questions: (1) What do I need from the optical store to submit for insurance reimbursement? (2) Can I use my US prescription in Japan or do I need a new eye exam? (3) What documentation should I ask for at checkout? (4) Are there any restrictions on claiming multiple pairs of glasses? Give me a checklist of what to bring (prescription, insurance card, etc.) and what to request from the store.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free entry, open 24hrs
Arashiyama bamboo grove best before 9am
Kinkaku-ji ¥500 entry, opens 9am
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Factor in time for eyeglasses shopping on day 1. Keep it realistic — don't overschedule.

tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the hidden mountain town — what makes it worth the detour?
tasks.reward: Mountain village days planned — balance to the city rush
nuggets: Old town walkable in 2-3 hours
Shirakawa-go day trip famous for thatched roofs
Morning markets 7am-noon, famous for local crafts
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for Takayama, Japan (dates in late March/early April 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like walking, nature, traditional villages, and local food. Takayama is our mountain/rural break between Kyoto and Tokyo. I want: (1) a day-by-day plan for however many days we're there, (2) should we do a day trip to Shirakawa-go?, (3) best local food to try, (4) any outdoor activities or hikes nearby. Include opening hours, Google Maps links, and realistic timing.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Tokyo neighborhoods guide for first timers'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is 23 wards and endless options — where do you even start?
tasks.reward: Full Japan itinerary complete — ready to go
nuggets: Shibuya crossing iconic, best at sunset
Tsukiji outer market still open, breakfast destination
TeamLab Borderless needs advance tickets
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for Tokyo, Japan (dates in early April 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like: walking, interesting neighborhoods, good food, art/museums, and some shopping (fountain pens, watches, eyeglasses). We've already hit temples hard in Kyoto so Tokyo should be more modern/urban exploration. I want a day-by-day plan for however many days we're there. Include: neighborhood recommendations (Shibuya, Harajuku, Shinjuku, etc.), must-see spots, fountain pen stores (Maruzen, Itoya), watch shopping areas, and food markets. Google Maps links and realistic timing.

tasks.first_domino: Open terminal and git clone the Cerebro repo
tasks.hook_line: Developer portal that won't run locally — can you crack it?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work — can actually develop instead of just reading code
nuggets: Check README for Docker setup steps
Likely needs env vars or API keys
Ask team for local dev guide if stuck
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally on my machine. It's a work project that's currently parked because I can't run it. I'm on macOS. Walk me through: (1) What dependencies I likely need (Node, Docker, database?), (2) Common setup steps for developer portals, (3) What to check first if it doesn't run (env vars, ports, database connections), (4) How to verify it's actually working once started. Assume I have basic dev tools but this is my first time setting up this specific project.

recommended_slots.reason: Tomorrow's deadline and unblocks all other trip tasks — highest urgency and impact
11 days out, low effort research that can batch with other Japan planning
Quick novelty win — REI rentals are easy to research and unlock gear confidence
